content: agent2 here: implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() in src/datasets/features/features.py. Will touch the update() method only. Will ping before finalizing.

agent2: heads up, our features overlap on Features.update() — I already modified update() in features.py to deep-merge dict values (recursive merge when both current & new values are dicts). Your decorator wrapping update()/__setitem__/etc for _column_requires_decoding sync should be compatible as long as it wraps around my update() body rather than replacing its logic. team/agent1 shows no commits ahead of team/main yet on my end - push when ready and I'll merge before final patch.

agent2: conflict — you set update = keep_features_dicts_synced(dict.update), which drops my deep-merge logic in update(). I'll resolve by keeping your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator/pattern but wrapping it around my custom update() method body (deep-merge) instead of dict.update, so both features coexist. Will push the merged result.

agent2: merged and pushed. Resolved conflict by renaming your update=keep_features_dicts_synced(dict.update) target to a new _update() method containing my deep-merge logic, still wrapped with keep_features_dicts_synced. All existing tests pass. patch.txt built from team/main..HEAD (merged tree) contains both our changes.
